div.head: The Chief of the American Delegation at the Meeting of the Advisory Committee on Traffic in Opium (Porter) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Statement of positionEnclosure 1 to instruction to the American Delegation, May 10, supra. with cabled addition in support of resolutions numbers one and twoEnclosure 2 to instruction to the American Delegation, May 10, supra. delivered this morning. Bishop Brent made a forceful and brilliant statement preceding my statement of the United States position.The complete texts of the statements by Bishop Brent and Mr. Porter may be found in the League of Nations publication, Advisory Committee on Traffic in Opium and Other Dangerous Drugs: Minutes of the Fifth Session, etc. (C. 418. M. 184. 1923. XI), pp. 10 ff. He discussed the moral aspects of the opium traffic and called upon the nations to suppress it without regard to the revenue derived therefrom, referring to our course in the Philippines.
